_comment: Creator OS connector registry + feature-flag contract. Each connector declares the normalized EVIDENCE it can provide so an atom can use whatever is available and DEGRADE via alternates when one is off. Offline contract only — we build NO provider auth/API clients here. Live retrieval is realized by the host AI's native integration (Claude / OpenAI / Gemini) when the deployment has connected it, or via manual_paste / uploaded_file. A deployment may RESTRICT specific evidence even when a connector is active (see per-deployment 'restricted_evidence' + reason); atoms then take what they can get and look elsewhere. Prose: connectors.md. Per-deployment flags: feature-flags.example.json.

connectors.label: Manual paste
connectors.auth_note: none — creator pastes data directly into the conversation

connectors.label: Uploaded file (read via shared/docintel/)
connectors.auth_note: none — creator uploads .pdf/.docx/.csv/.xlsx; read via docintel-engine and parse_text.py

connectors.label: Local .local.json data files (voice-profile, channel-context, content-calendar)
connectors.auth_note: none — reads pipeline/user-context/*.local.json; gitignored, never committed. Created by tools/setup.py.

connectors.label: YouTube Data API v3
connectors.auth_note: OAuth 2.0 or API key via Google Cloud Console. Quota: 10,000 units/day default. Realized by host AI's native Google integration or via integrations-engine.md credential pattern. Enable by setting youtube_api: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Instagram Graph API (Meta) v25.0
connectors.auth_note: Instagram Business or Creator account required. OAuth 2.0 via Meta app. Scopes: instagram_business_basic, instagram_business_content_publish, instagram_business_manage_insights. Enable by setting instagram_api: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: TikTok APIs (Content Posting, Research, Display)
connectors.auth_note: TikTok developer account required. Login Kit for OAuth 2.0. Content Posting API: video.publish or video.upload scope. Research API: non-profit/academic only. Enable by setting tiktok_api: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Pinterest API v5
connectors.auth_note: Pinterest Developer account required. OAuth 2.0. Scopes: pins:read, pins:write, boards:read. Rate limits: reads 1,000/min, writes 100/min. Enable via creator-os-config.local.json (no capability flag exists yet — add pinterest_api: true).

connectors.label: Google Drive / Google Workspace
connectors.auth_note: Realized by host AI's native Google Drive integration when the deployment has connected it. Scopes: drive.readonly for reading files. No client built here.

connectors.label: Offline SQLite caches (keyword FTS5 + competitor snapshots)
connectors.auth_note: none — reads shared/cache/index.local.db (FTS5 keyword cache) and pipeline/competitor-snapshots/index.local.db (competitor metadata). Both gitignored. Built by tools/setup.py and tools/competitor_snapshot.py.

connectors.label: Creator OS MCP server (stdio, Claude Desktop)
connectors.auth_note: none — stdio transport. Requires pip install mcp + claude_desktop_config.json entry. Exposes 8 tools: cache_query, competitor_scan, source_staleness, drift_check, quality_score, add_competitor, get_capabilities, get_connectors. Enable by setting mcp_server: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Playwright Chromium (JS rendering for competitor snapshots)
connectors.auth_note: pip install -r requirements-render.txt + playwright install chromium. On macOS M2: auto-downloads arm64 binary. Enables ytInitialData extraction from YouTube pages that require JavaScript. Enable by setting playwright: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Web crawl (acquire.py + fetch_resilient.py, polite rate-governed)
connectors.auth_note: none — pip install -r requirements-crawl.txt + requirements-scraper.txt. Rate governor respects robots.txt and Crawl-delay. No API key required for basic crawl. Always available if requirements-crawl.txt is installed.
